reviews.text: I'm delighted to share my positive experience working with Mr. Koon as my lawyer. His expertise and dedication made a significant difference in resolving my legal matters. Each step along the way they made sure I was taken care of, from scheduling doctors appointments to follow-ups all the way through to finally getting my settlement check from the insurance company. I appreciate their professionalism, patience and thoroughness. Today, because of this team, I can finally rest easy knowing my financial burdens that's been keeping me up at night are no longer a concern. You can't put a price to that. Thank you so much!

I really appreciated James taking on my case and being honest and upfront about the expectations and process since it was a rather unusual personal injury case. I was 36 weeks pregnant when a large metal framed sign fell down and cut my head and wrist open while I was shopping. I am very thankful to James and his team because I needed the help of a personal injury lawyer to get my medical bills taken care of and teach this company a lesson in being negligent. The team kept me informed every step of the way and I was always able to receive a response from them within hours of sending an email.

This firm have been my support and saving grace throughout my car accident case. Needless to say, I highly recommend this law firm. From the initial FREE consultation meet and greet, to the numerous communications through email, calls and text, they were to listen, give me advise and work diligently to settle this case. They explained every new terms to me (which are all legal terms that I cannot understand), allowing me to make the right decisions. I couldn't thank this team of highly skilled professionals enough, what I can only do is to provide them an over-top rating!

I felt very comfortable working with her as my attorney. Needless to say, they gave me peace of mind throughout this initimidating process and I would highly reccommend them to others.

When I decided to divorce my ex she pulled something disgusting and low landing me in the crosshairs of the law for the first time in my life. Hiring Andy was the best decision I have ever made. He not only carried me thru this ordeal but did so with patience, superior knowledge of the law, above and beyond customer service, and professional excellence. It’s stressful enough to get arrested and dealing with courts and police but hiring Andy was a godsend for someone who deals with anxiety on a daily basis. If something happens to you or you are in legal trouble do yourself and your family a favor and choose Andy, you will NOT regret it for a second. Oh and if he gives you a thumbs up while you are in court you won 😉

In April of 2022, I was arrested for defending my home and property from a subject who was refusing to leave my residence. Upon my release, I first tried to handle the matter through the Public Defender's Office. Weeks went by with no sort of contact and it did not appear as though the Public Defender had my best interests in mind. After enough frustration, I finally contacted the Dunham & Ingram Office and I wish I had done it sooner! Mr.Ingram acted swiftly and professionally. He reassured me and helped dispel any concerns I had. He also went through with me and explained step by step how the process would go. When I sent emails to his office or called them on the phone, there was always a friendly voice on the other side that was willing to speak to me. Mr.Ingram responded to my emails promptly and timely on every occasion. Mr.Ingram conducted his research of my case and found that I acted within accordance of the law and subsequently filed a "Motion to Dismiss". Within 3 days, the state attorney's office had dismissed all of the charges against me. Andy did more to help me within a week, than the public defender did within a month. If you find yourself in a terrible situation like I did, call Mr.Ingram. This is your life and livelihood at risk. I can't thank him enough for the services he offered to me.
He is very professional and knowledgeable. During the whole process he was confident and because of that I was not stress thru it. Thanks for everything

The entire staff is thoughtful and empathetic and Mr. Dunham is a knowledgeable and effective litigator who also is able to address 'real world' concerns of his clients and produce practical solutions beyond litigation.

I was looking for an attorney to represent me during a dissolution of marriage case when I called upon Dunham and Ingram.  Attorney Chris Dunham agreed to take on my case. Chris was very helpful in pointing out things to protect me as the process ensued and would return calls and meet  when needed. The wife and I were able to come to an agreement which was acceptable to both parties and documented by Mr. Dunham.  From start to finish it took approximately three months to complete.  I am extremely glad Mr. Dunham handled this case for me. I will recommend him to others and will not hesitate to use his services should the need arise in the future.

When I was first looking to hire an attorney, I made contact with Dunham and Ingram first.  They gave me great advice on my phone consultation and encouraged me to shop around and interview several firms and individuals.  I went with my gut and the firm that gave me the best feeling and chose to hire Andy Ingram as my attorney.  What I soon discovered is that Andy Ingram is amazing.  I hired him to represent me in my case and was amazed with his results.  Mr. Ingram worked on my case and kept me fully abreast of every development, the strategies for fighting my case and took the time to explain to me what everything meant.  He was very mindful of my wallet and made the effort to exercise fiscal responsibility and not just see dollar signs. If you have a criminal case then I would encourage you to seek the guidance and expertise of Mr. Ingram.  You will not be sorry that you did, if there is a positive that can come from your situation, he will find it and achieve it.

I recently hired Mr. Andy Ingram and I would most definitely recommend him to friends and family.  Mr. Ingram is very professional and great at what he does.  I was very nervous for my first time in front of a judge, but Mr. Ingram helped me relax and assured me that everything would turn out okay.  Thank you, Mr. Ingram!!!

Attorney Andy Ingram was born with a special gift to be a criminal attorney.  He is not just a good attorney, he is the best.  And, juries seem to love him!  I was charged by the State in Marion County w leaving the scene of an accident. It sounds awful but there was no accident on the highway or road, this was at a ratty little Diamond Oil gas station, and someone saw me coming!  Among others, I had to go up against manufactured lies claiming that I banged into a car, lies about people who weren't even there, and trumped up charges of a FHP officer pressured to make an arrest at any cost. On top of that the State's main witness has a criminal history.  Thank you Andy for staving off all of the lame theories the State accused me of, and, for making the State look small and stupid.  I also thank the jury system.  The gas station involved has been documented for skimmer activity @ the pumps.  It seems no one tells the truth on the stand anymore so that is especially why one needs the best attorney possible, and especially in Marion County, Florida!
Performed beyond my expectations! Easy to talk to and understand Would definitely call them again if I needed an attorney

I highly recommend Dunham & Ingram as your professional attorney. Andy is not just very professional but is also very reliable. I hired him two days before the appearance of my son's case with ease of knowing that he would take care of his case for me. He is a fantastic attorney and you've made the right choice if you go with there law firm.  He is very personable and really does want the best out come for his clients. Thank you again Andy for helping me with my son's case.

I hired Mr. Andy Ingram to represent my case in a highly delicate situation affecting my Son and he did an amazing job for me, Mr. Ingram's professionalism with handling my case was what everyone hopes to obtain when retaining an attorney. His knowledge of the law combined with having myself and my family's best interest in mind eased the pain through a difficult process.

Mr. Ingram and his Firm do not waste money with frivolous email, calls or other forms of communication and spent my dollars wisely. He was extremely knowledgeable regarding laws and how it pertained to my Son’s case.  He was on top of everything and I could not have been happier.
He kept me informed and was available when I needed him. His intelligent approach to law commands respect.
If you want an attorney that has your back, I strongly recommend Andy Ingram to represent you.

Yours truly,

Paula Fahey

I recently hired Mr. Ingram after a misunderstanding between my husband and his PO, which resulted in a VOP. He helped tremendously with what could've been an extremely stressful and devastating situation for my family. Not only did he expedite the hearing process, but he somehow convinced the judge to let my husband come home without any further time served. Our family will be forever grateful for his hard work! Best money I've ever spent, hiring Andy! Thank you so, so much!

Recently I was arrested for a crime. I thought it would be easy to navigate the system with a public defender who only wanted me to take a plea deal. With only 2 weeks to go to trial I contacted Andy Ingram. Immediatly he took my case and won a not guilty plea at trial. Andy is sharp. He knows the law, loves the law and believes in the law and it shows in his compassion as well as the persuit of the truth. Andy was the only one who looked for the truth in my case and exposed it. I don't plan on being in a legal situation again but if I am, he will be my lawyer. I cannot thank him enough and I highly recommend him.

Mr. Ingram helped me out in a tough situation when I felt I had no where else to turn. He made me aware of all of my options while remaining professional and surprisingly understandable. If you are unsure that you require his services I recommend that you call him and set up an appointment just to see what your options are. You wont regret it.

Mr. Ingram is not only good at what he does but he is also a good person. He helped me when I needed it the most. He went above and beyond to make sure that everything was taken care of for my case. I really recommend Dunham and Ingram if anyone ever need help this is the place to go. Don't be afraid to call them even if it is just to see if you need council they're very easy to talk to and not intimating to speak with.

well I can say Mr. Ingram is good attorney and also a good man at heart .well with me he went and really help me . I was truly worried when I needed help responding to an appeal, but that changed the moment I met with Ms. Sabina and her team. From the start, Sabina was incredibly kind, knowledgeable, and patient. She answered every question I had and guided me through each step of the process with care and professionalism. I left her office feeling confident and reassured, knowing I had everything I needed. I’m so grateful for her support and would wholeheartedly recommend her to anyone in need. Thank you, Ms. Sabina, for your help and dedication!

Sabina and her staff are extremely knowledgeable and helpful. They carefully explained each step of the divorce process, and Sabina helped me navigate through difficult choices. Jessica, Sherri, and Joyce are quick to respond and kind. Also, there’s always chocolate in the lobby. Sabina says that chocolate makes everything better, and it certainly helps. I would highly recommend Home Town Law for divorce proceedings.
Sherri went above and beyond ìn getting me taken care of. Her professionalism was astounding. She deserves a raise.

I cannot explain correctly or completely how thankful I am to have had Taren by my side representing me in my divorce. Every step of the long, stressful and emotionally exhausting process, she was there, calm, understanding, supportive, and reminding me to take care of myself. While so much of a marriage is private and deeply personal, sharing it all with Taren so not only she could understand, but use it to build my case to fully reflect who I was as a wife to my ex-husband, I never felt judged or like she didn’t care. She slowly rebuilt the confidence that I had lost in myself and in humanity.

The day of our trial, which for over a year I had been dreading, due to the anxiety of being in the same room with my ex-husband, losing my growing self esteem, facing the fact that the life we’d built together for a decade-  was over and every single flaw and failure on both sides was about to be aired. Taren alongside her assistant, Taj,  kept me grounded. I was reminded to breathe, that just because our marriage failed-  I was not a failure, and that no matter what I would walk out divorced and free to start living again.

Taren made what I had been so terrified of since the papers were served- the day of my marriage legally ending and my entire imagined future turning to dust, into the day I felt the most seen and supported. Every facet of our life together was exposed and instead of feeling ashamed that I couldn’t make work, I finally understood what she’d had been trying to show me. The marriage wasn’t a good marriage, and ending it was an unburdening.

I walked out divorced, truly happy, and thankful to have met someone who saw me better than I could see myself.

Taren, if you read this, thank you. As someone who I’m sure wasn’t easy to represent, all factors considered, you advocated for me better than anyone else possibly could. You do not know how much you personally have affected my life, even outside of my divorce. You are who humans should strive to become.
Very awesome sauce 👍
Great
Great attorney. Thank you for helping 👍🏼

reviews.text:
Brian Schackow was recommended to me by my oldest son Kodey. When Kodey was involved in a traffic accident, Brian fought for him and helped him receive a generous settlement.

Unfortunately accidents happen and my accident was a head on car crash that resulted in multiple fractures of my lower left leg. I was in bad shape, had multiple surgeries and really didn’t feel like messing with all the legalities of an accident. In stepped Brian and I never worried again. He was supportive and listened to the concerns of my wife and I. He was extremely knowledgeable and walked me through every step of a process that without him, I would be aggravated, frustrated and truly lost. He stood up for me at every juncture. He fought for me when I couldn’t. I truly appreciate Brian and his entire team. From working with insurance companies to hospital reps Brian surpassed my expectations. I thank you so much! If anyone experiences an accident, I would highly recommend Schackow and Mercadante...specifically Brian! Thank you again!

Chris took my wife’s case knowing that it was going to be difficult and if he lost he wouldn’t be paid. Thankfully through prayer and his hard work she was judged fully favorable for social security disability.

Mr. Annis was a great help in my husband's disability case.  He was so prompt and very patient with all of our questions.  Mr. Annis gave us clear expectations and directives that helped my husband be awarded his disability.  The office staff is wonderful!  We would highly recommend his services!

I never felt ignored. I would definitely recommend Mr. Annis to anyone that wants a lawyer that cares.i would also like to add that he has a really beautifully decorated office. I strongly recommend Chris Annis to take care of your legal matters. Thank you so much Chris.

He help me understand the paperwork explain things I didn't know. It wasn't stressful, he was very understanding.  So if you looking for a good lawyer who is a good listern and like collad green and buttermilk pie LoL.go see Mr. Christopher

Mr. Annis is a fighter for his clients and pushes for what they deserve. I appreciate Mr. Annis's entire firm, they treat you like family from beginning to end.

Brett represented my company very professionally and worked hard to achieve a positive outcome.  His firm communicated well and kept us up to date with the progress of our case.

Mr. Little was the attorney appointed to my daughter/myself by our insurance company.

My daughter and I am very  thankful for him handling our case. We will always be grateful to him for all the work he did.   I highly recommend Brett Little, Esquire.
Brett Little did a good job helping my elderly mother in a driving court case.  He was so kind, always approachable and always kept our family informed.  .

10/10 I would use Carolina again.  She was more worried about helping me get a result than she was about how much work she had to do.  Big law firms make you feel like a number, Carolina’s law office is big enough to get a good result and small enough that you can talk to her on the phone.

Carolina is excellent. She speaks English and Spanish, which made everything much easier for my Spanish-speaking family. Carolina is also very thorough, flexible, great communicating, and most importantly: she genuinely cares about her clients!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for leaving the kind words. It means a lot to me.

reviews.text: I've closed a few transactions with Maury and Ray. Their support staff is competent, reliable and responsive. As a REALTOR®, it's important that I have real estate attorneys I can rely on. They do a great job.

Biggest waste of money I have ever invested in a law firm. Was charged an insane amount of money ($10,000 on top of the $7500 retainer fee) for my attorney, Kevin Jerucko, to become familiar with my case. Then, I was charged more money when I had to meet with him in person to familiarize him with the case! It was shocking and disheartening to find out he didn’t even know basic facts about the current order, my ex, or ongoing litigation. I highly recommend you save your money and hire a different law firm. Sometimes spending double the money does not equate to better representation.
Zero results with 17k of billing. Kirsten should be sent for a Fl Bar review, which I am in the process of filing a complaint with.

Avoid This Law Firm at All Costs - Utterly Incompetent and Unethical

This firm is a disgrace to the legal profession. In 2014, they placed a lien on my property. One of their attorneys has since passed away, and now they claim to have no record of the lien or the payment--yet I'm the one left to clean up their mess while trying to sell my property. Instead of taking responsibility, they pass the burden onto the victim, forcing me to hire another attorney to resolve their incompetence.

Stay far away from these people unless you want legal nightmares instead of solutions.
They get it done

I had an excellent meeting with Ramona Chance today.  I found her manner very professional and was very pleased with her knowledge of Real Estate Law.
Amazing woman, lazer focused! Definitely a great person to have looking out for your best interest !!!

She called me back within minutes and explained to me just what I needed and what the price would be.  The work was started that very day. While waiting for other parties who were involved, she kept me well-informed. Everything was done in a very timely and professional manner. I will definitely use her services again and I would highly recommend her.

My estate attorney recommended Ramona for a document he said I needed regarding the leaving of my property to my heirs.  I called Ramona's office expecting to make an appointment for that and was surprised when the receptionist put Ramona herself on the phone. In other words, she turned down paid work because she didn't think it was right for me.  I think that is great.  If I ever need a real estate attorney, I will be calling her.
Justin and his team were wonderful to work with.  He got the best possible outcome for both myself and my children.  Highly recommend!!
Justin was super to work with. He’s very knowledgeable and worked diligently for a favorable outcome. Highly recommend!

After extensive online research, I decided to contact Justin to be my divorce attorney. Gainesville is my home, but the nature of my work requires I travel most of the time, normally overseas, and I needed  an attorney that would understand the complexities of my situation and work with me to get a speedy divorce that would not affect my work.  Justin and his team were not only professional and compassionate, but they patiently guided me through the process, accommodating my time-zone differences  and delays, making sure no detail was left unattended, and with no time wasted in court, allowing the divorce to not turn contentious. Justin was clear, responsive, and  a true advocate for me, which sometimes meant he had to tell me things I didn't want to hear, but always did so in a manner that was professional and kind.  I wholeheartedly recommend Justin.

Justin did a fantastic job with my divorce. He patiently guided me through every step of the process, worked out a good settlement for me, and did not charge anything excessive.  I honestly could not have asked for a better attorney, especially given that divorce is one of the worst things one can go through on one’s life. I would choose Justin again with no hesitation, and I would recommend him to anyone seeking a highly competent and thoughtful attorney.

I chose Justin to represent me in my divorce after some extensive online searching. Just has been nothing but up front and honest in all dealings, even telling me things I did not want to hear, but did so in a manner that made sense all while answering all of my questions. Justin is nothing but professional and was really easy to talk to about issues. 10/10 would hire again for my next divorce (kidding of course).

Review for Justin Jacobson, Bogin, Munns & Munns

I had the privilege of working with Justin Jacobson over the course of a difficult, four-year divorce that spanned two states. Justin’s professionalism, efficiency, and strategic command of both legal procedure and financial details made an otherwise overwhelming process manageable. He is incredibly gifted at crafting responses, anticipating issues, and navigating complicated situations with clarity and precision.

His calm, steady presence and deep knowledge provided reassurance every step of the way. Equally impressive was his office staff — consistently friendly, empathetic, and efficient. They always made me feel heard and supported, no matter how complex or emotional the situation.

I am deeply grateful for Justin and his team. I would highly recommend him to anyone in need of a skilled, compassionate, and dedicated attorney

I would love to change this review, but right now I have some time-dependent things that I'm now scrambling to find a different lawyer for.  If he's not taking new clients, the firm should have let me know!

Mr. Jacobson has worked on my dissolution of marriage case. During the whole process Justin showed patience, he was always available or returning my calls shortly, provided great advices and consultaion. It was very hard to find a good lawyer to work on my case in Gainesville area, so I highly recommend Mr. Justin Jacobson as he is an great and knowledgeable family lawyer.

Divorce can be a daunting experience, especially for men. Justin's knowledge and experience in family law made the process much smoother. reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your feedback. We take all client concerns seriously and strive to provide attentive and professional representation to every individual who contacts our office.

While we cannot discuss the specific details of any matter in a public forum due to confidentiality obligations, we can say that our firm makes multiple efforts to communicate with clients and to move cases forward in a timely manner. In some situations, when required information or cooperation is not received despite repeated requests, we may determine that we are unable to continue representation in a way that meets our professional standards and ethical obligations.

Our decision to withdraw from any case is never made lightly and is done only after careful consideration and in accordance with applicable rules. We remain committed to treating every client with professionalism and respect, and we expect the same in return to ensure the best possible outcome.

We wish you the very best moving forward.

After not hearing anything after a day and half I emailed him “ how are we doing need to tell my buyer something”? No Response.

I was very concerned about loosing my buyer because he didn’t understand why I couldn’t come up with a simple agreement. Bryan disappeared and has totally ignored my emails which is totally disrespectful and unprofessional. If he was to busy or for whatever reason didn’t want to handle it, all he had to do was say so. That would have provided me the opportunity to move on to another firm which I have done. Unfortunately I lost 3 days and are now approaching the holidays
which everyone will be closed.

For the record, the reason I always emailed him is because he does not accept phone calls to his office and demands email correspondences which his answering machine states. reviews.text:
There are not enough good things I can say about Mrs. Budnik.  My wife and I brought her onto a case after losing faith in a different lawyer, when our "slam-dunk" easement case seemed to falter.  She came in, took control, and brought us through trial with amazing efficiency and results.  She has the courage and integrity to tell her clients when they need to let things go, and what to focus on to achieve their goals in the case.  She is on of the best procedural lawyers I’ve ever seen.  Pricing is commensurate with her skill and she seemed to bill less time than she actually worked on my case.

If you need a lawyer, she should be your first call and it would behoove you to follow her advice.

We encountered an issue that required legal assistance in Gainesville. We reached out to several attorneys in the area, and received little to no response. Ultimately, we contacted Carolyn Budnik's firm. We were facing a foreclosure redemption issue as a buyer and needed legal representation.

Initially, we thought the situation would be resolved quickly, but it turned out to be quite complex and drawn out.

Fortunately, we had the Budnik Law Firm on our side. Ms. Budnik was dedicated, responsive, and earned our trust completely. Her rates were very reasonable. We would have faced exorbitant costs had we chosen another attorney.

We are forever grateful for all the hard work she put into our case. She was able to complete the redemption for us, and also referred us to another attorney when the owner of the property would not vacate.

If you need a knowledgeable and trustworthy attorney, we would gladly recommend Carolyn Budnik.
Carolyn is a straight shooter and will always tell it like it is.  She was instrumental in helping me with questions about refinancing my home.

reviews.text:
Story Time : My credit card rental insurance which was supposed to cover the damage to my rental car denied the claim using baseless grounds. We had tried everything we could to resolve the issue with them , but they were just stubborn because they didn't want to pay up. We were frustrated and clueless about how to proceed. We called up a lot of firms in the Gainesville area. Almost all of them refused to take up the case because there was no personal injury involved (since there's not much money in it for them without personal injury).

And then there's Schackow & Mercadante. I left them an email to which Teresa at their office promptly responded. Brian was the first attorney who actually  took the time to look at the details of the case thoroughly. Teresa got back to me saying we have a strong case, and advised that they'd send a letter to the credit card company on my behalf demanding coverage. I did not even have to meet them in person. All communication was very prompt and over email. They sent the letter. The credit card company covered the entire damages. Saved me from paying around 30,000 dollars with absolutely no hassle! It was such a huge relief. Shout-out to Teresa and Brian! I can't thank them enough! :)

Now, Schackow and Mercadante could've decided to take it to court and drive up the legal fees. They didn't. These are just super-honest folks who genuinely care about their clients. They're definitely not in the business just for the money. They want to make a difference in the world and they're extremely good and very professional at what they do. And I was pleasantly surprised to see that!
reviews.ownerResponse: Delighted that Schackow & Mercadante could resolve your issue with professionalism and care. Teresa and Brian truly exemplify our commitment to client satisfaction.

I had a complicated case that started 3 and half years ago.  I was uncertain if I could find an attorney who would hear me and understand the intricacies of the events. When I reached out to Brian Schackow he agreed to meet with me.  I found Brian and Teresa to be very understanding, compassionate and also very clear and knowledgeable about the law. It was a long process of waiting patiently and understanding that results take time and all parties have a certain amount of time to respond.  My case wasn't huge and he could have easily refused to take on the case.  However, he stuck with me and we all waited patiently for events to unfold.  Last week, my case was settled and I feel as though, under the circumstances,  the best outcome was achieved. I really appreciate Brian and Teresa for their kindness and communication throughout the whole process.  I felt heard and responded to gently but also clearly and confidently. I feel I can now move on with my life in a positive way.  Thank you.

John Middleton knows this individual...
I know of a Military Veteran who was arrested in 2006 for interference with child custody and was put in the Llano County Jail for 180 days. He literally seized his daughter and refused to return her back to her mother as decreed by the court. That's pretty audacious. After 6 months, it was determined that the actions by the Dad were justifiable. He was then extradited from Texas back to Florida for violation of parole for his arrest of interference with child custody. This guy was on parole as a violent convicted felon as an accessory with 3 others involved in the shooting of a person. According to Parole Commission mitigating Factors in his case, it found mitigating circumstances that he did in fact plead for the victim's Life. Why the Court did not choose to consider that fact is unknown. It was at a time when the State of Florida (1970's) began to Industrialize its prison system and were going after as many convictions as possible.
After a review by a Florida parole examiner with the parole commission, the parole examiner found him not guilty for the parole violation of interference with child custody, and recommended to the parole commission that his parole be reinstated without further delay for the sake of his Daughter. January 2007, he was then released back on parole and after 7 months, the state of Texas gave him lawful custody of his then 6 year old daughter without regards to geographic location. How is that possible? How... is... that... possible??? He's a violent convicted felon at least that's how he's labeled on paper. You just don't give custody of a little girl to a convicted violent felon on parole. That's entirely against everything the judicial system strives for.
He was the only violent convicted felon on parole in the State of Florida to ever have custody of a child especially a little girl.
The only logical conclusion that it was even possible, is that God arranged that himself... That's how that was possible.
There's something more to this story and this guy than what meets the eye. A possible good guy being labeled as a bad guy? Why?
John will not help this guy to have his firearm rights restored. All of his other rights have been restored. He's been discharged from parole for 10 years.

Tough Love!
My case was unique, as I was dealing with a little known, and  woefully misdiagnosed, misunderstood obscure illness that was slowly destroying every nerve and fiber in my body, but on the outside, I looked great, which is the reason I could not get the help I needed; that is until I met Attorney Dunmore.
At first, I didn't now how to read Atty Dunmore; upon our first meeting she was frank, even gruff I would say, but not rude or egotistical. She was tough and wanted the facts, yet she was also very patient, but not the type to suffer maudlin ramblings.
I explained my situation to her - how I had been let down by another atty, and also let down by many doctors and medical professionals, I was angry, distrusting, and jaded by the entire process - and while sharing my story with her, she would break in to keep me on point, which irked me a little, but after a time it slowly dawned on me that this person was the real deal; she was the perfect professional to go up against the system of gate keepers on my behalf!  I needed someone like Atty Dunmore to sort me out, to help me stop looking back, to help me trust, and get with the program so we could win this battle. And Atty Dunmore did just that!  The hearing went very well, and I am now, and will continue to be receiving the assistance I need.
Looking back I feel blessed that she put up with me, and am grateful for the effort she made to D-R-A-G me into reality, while educating me on the system processes so I can succeed!  A very good person, an erudite professional, and a very great attorney!

reviews.text: I wanted to take a moment to share my experiences regarding being a client at Glassman and Zissimopulos with John Kelly as my attorney. It was initially successful until John Kelly came into the case. Even with the trauma and shock I was immediately put at ease with Mr Kelly’s presence and calm professional demeanor. He clearly knows and understands the law and I learned a lot as this case moved forward for a grueling several weeks. My life and the lives of my family hung in the balance while my whole family struggled with the injustice that was done to me and my children. Mr Kelly did not rush our meetings and took the time to educate and assure me of the plan moving forward. The first time seeing him in action in court in front of the judge was truly impressive. Even with all the uncertainty I became aware as my fate hung in the balance that it was all going to be ok. I cannot thank this man enough for all his efforts, hard work and knowledge in the law. reviews.text:
I highly advise against consulting with this firm if you are in need of any type of representation regarding family law as she is turning away cases and “cherry picking” cases based on her own preference and not based a potential client’s needs.
In addition, I am an associate of Jana H who also left a review and I can attest to the fact that she followed all instructions, and was told she now had representation when in reality, her calls were ignored and only returned by office staff. Meanwhile she was berated by her soon to be ex husbands lawyer and continually made attempts to speak directly with Ms McNeal who pretty much left her on “read”.
It’s heartbreaking to watch people in need, be strung along while they wait for assistance and are left out to dry by a professional who took money, and gave verbal confirmation that she had accepted the case. I am appalled at the response left by McNeal, who shows no remorse or compassion for a potential client and even goes so far as to insinuate any of this was to the fault of the potential client.
reviews.ownerResponse:
YOU have never personally used our services. Further, part of the advice we give to all clients is to not disclose discussions between lawyer and client to any 3rd parties. Clearly, by your post, your friend failed to follow that advice, potentially waiving attorney/client privilege. Your "review" then opens up the potential of her spouse getting said information. So, probably not the wisest choice to leave such a review.
Further, yes, as a private business, we can and do select which clients we want based on the facts of the case, and which ones we don't. Clearly, we made the right decision in your friend's case.

reviews.text:
I called to retain Kelly McNeal a couple months after I did a paid consultation for a divorce. Her front desk person said she would call me back. After not hearing from her for a day I called and was told she had accepted my case and she was getting my paperwork together. After waiting for almost 3 weeks with two messages left that were never returned, I called and the same person told me she was not sure if she was going to take my case and wanted additional information. I was taken back but answered her questions. I’m now telling a front desk person personal info about my case which felt very unprofessional and made me uncomfortable. To my surprise I received a call back the next day! Wow, they finally called me back but to say she was “straying away from family law and was cherry picking her cases”. I was devastated. I asked please for my consultation money back for she willing took it as she could take my case and now that she is not will she please return my money. I have called twice about this refund with NO response.
Going through a divorce is terrible but the way this firm treated me was horrific. Let it be known if you are wanting a divorce attorney do not go here. I’m in the health care profession and I would never ever imagine treating one of my patients the way her front desk person treated me. Kelly McNeal Legal took my money and then refused to help me.
Kelly has never reached out to me on this matter despite numerous attempts from me. Her front desk staff is terrible. Very rude. Doesn’t return messages and even lied to me about my case.
I don’t want anyone to go through what I did with this firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: The Florida Bar limits what information we can utilize to reply.  With that in mind, this person sought our advice, was given advice, failed to follow it, and then, 3 months later, sought to hire us.  We do not take on clients that fail to follow our legal advice during and after a consultation.  We do not offer refunds for consultations that took place 3 months prior, where a case is reviewed, discussed, and legal advice is given.

reviews.text: I sought out McNeal Legal specifically based on their Google reviews. When I explained that I was looking for representation for an upcoming case and asked if I could set up a time for a consultation, I was told they charge $300 for that. When I questioned her and explained that I’m not looking for free legal advice, that I have money for a retainer, and that I’m simply trying to determine if they will be a good fit, she reiterated that they charge for that. JBC is a licensed general contractor servicing the SE and SW areas of South Florida. We provide services for both residential and commercial projects for concrete shell work. Sometimes we encounter issues that require legal assistance because as a corporation only an attorney can provide that legal help. Fortunately for JBC as the owner, I had a personal issue where my family and me needed an attorney and a good one. We stumbled onto this law firm and Brad Willard. If you see my previous personal post, you will see how absolutely brilliant Mr. Willard proved to be. We ended up in trial and as previously stated he destroyed the claims of the opposing party.
So when my company ran into a collection issue and after 3 months of trying to collect on my own getting no where I approached Mr. Willard for help. Understanding Mr. Willard is in Gainesville and my issue was in Hollywood, 300 miles to the south, I did not know if he would take on my case. After explaining my dilemma and asking for help Mr. Willard agreed to look into the matter.
Mr. Willard took immediate control, jumped into action and after filing a series of court documents, he accomplished what I couldn't do in three months in 14 days. Not only did he get their attention, he has secured a written agreement with the surety company obtaining the first of a series of payments that our company is owed. Not only has he secured the agreement, he secured the FULL amount owed to JBC,  and he recovered our FULL attorneys fees. ALL IN 14 DAYS of filing our complaint in court. Brilliantly written, brilliantly executed and brilliantly negotiated. This is my second case with Mr. Willard, not only does he bring a wealth of trial attorney experience, his ability to adapt to a variety of case work is impressive. I want to publicly thank Brad Willard for a job EXTREMELY well done. My thanks to the law firm of
SALTER & FIEBER for having such talented attorneys in their firm. This firm and Brad Willard has earned not 5 stars but 10 stars from JBC.
They always provide excellent services. My real estate customers have always had great experiences when buying or selling homes.

We, (my family & me) found ourselves embroiled in a nasty dispute with a local neighbor in a very rural neighborhood. We purchased almost 24 acres more than 17 years ago to escape the confines of HOA' rules and regulations and nosy neighbors. We are ardent second amendment supporters, and we like our ATV's and Dirt Bikes. Hence, we bought enough property so we could enjoy our type of lifestyle. After more 16 years of doing just this without any interference, newcomers to the neighborhood moved in across the street and abruptly advised us that both our dirt bikes and guns were disturbing them and we needed to cease and desist our lifestyle. Footnote: We keep our property in pristine condition. This culminated in the neighbors calling the Marion County Sheriff's Office on us virtually every time we shoot our guns (more than 20 times). Notwithstanding, their property is more than 1700 feet or 3 full football fields away from where we shoot and in the opposite direction. This ultimately resulted in a repeat violence and stalking allegations filed against us in court.
We hired Mr. Brad Willard of Salter & Feiber law firm. Where do I begin?
1. Attentive 2. Talented 3. Available on a moments notice 4. Definitely Detailed oriented. 5. He listens to you.
But more than all of that, he literally destroyed our neighbors testimony. I mean DESTROYED our neighbor when he got him on the witness stand. He exposed his lies, his made up stories, and exposed him for exactly what he was trying to do. Our neighbor tried bringing New York City values and gun laws to rural Marion County. Mr. Willard protected us from him and those restrictions and for that we are most grateful to him and his law firm. Highly respected and Highly recommend. Mr. Willard is a true skilled trial attorney and brings a wealth of experience to his clients. I would not like to be on the other side of him in a court room!

reviews.ownerResponse:
We regret to hear that you didn't have a good experience with our firm. We take client satisfaction very seriously and are truly sorry that we were unable to take on your case. We strive to help everyone that we can, but there are many factors that go into determining whether we can take on a case. We do not turn down cases lightly. Florida has a very short statute of limitations for medical malpractice claims. Many people are unaware of the 2-year limitations. Unfortunately, when the malpractice occurs outside of the 2-year window, there is nothing that we can do in the legal sense in order to pursue a claim. Regrettably, we are forced to decline clients because the statute of limitations has expired.

We always strive to ensure clients understand the reasons we are not able to take on a case, but if you do not feel you received a sufficient explanation, please reach out to us so we can further discuss it with you. Again, we are sorry we were unable to take on your case.

reviews.text:
In June 2016 I was in an auto accident where another driver ran a stop sign. This was the just the beginning of what has been one of the toughest times in my adult life. After spending the next couple months in and out of hospitals - dealing with Doctors, specialists and PT learning to walk again the bills started rolling in. Not to mention, from the accident onward I couldn’t work for almost a year. I was increasingly frustrated because my insurance company was not talking to me and in addition not paying any of the claims being submitted. The person who caused the accident had insurance and they were claiming we were somehow at fault. Things were not going well and I didn’t know where to turn.

A friend of mine recommended I call Farah & Farah. I had absolutely no experience in this type of stuff so I took their advice and called the number I got online. This was hands down the best decision I made during this whole process. Kristine Anderson the case manager from the local office called me back and was very caring and understanding. She assured me the insurance company would be responding to me very quickly after she called them and things would begin looking up. She was absolutely right. I guess they know her and know she doesn’t play around. I received a call from my insurance company that afternoon assuring me they would be taking care of me in short order.  While that wasn’t exactly the truth, Ms Anderson kept her promise and never let up on them.

The Attorney’s legal assistant is Whitney Dyess and I can not say enough great things about her. She is nice, polite, understanding, and extremely organized. There were many time I called her on a bad day and she calmed me down and I felt better after the call. She is great to deal with.

I saved the attorney I had for last. Charles Bowen is a cool dude. He is a young, hungry and smart attorney. He knows the tips along with the in’s and out’s of this process. When he and Kristine started working with the insurance companies they started paying claims, also the hospitals and health care providers started reducing their ridiculously large medical bills.

Mr Bowen is as someone explained him - good people- as are Whitney and Kristine.

All in all- I hope to never need an attorney again, but if I do I will be calling Charles Bowen’s team at the Gainesville Farah & Farah office. They helped make a bad situation better.

reviews.ownerResponse: Our office tries very hard to return every phone call to our office. My assistant listens to all our voice messages every day. If you did not receive a call back, it is very likely that there was a problem with your message such as your cell phone cutting out and we couldn't hear your message. Our office does not discriminate against anyone; in fact, we make a serious effort to counter the effects of discrimination in our society by providing pro bono or reduced fees to people in need. You were not a client in our office, and we do not have a record of your call. I would ask you to remove your negative review as you were not a client and are not therefore in a position to judge the quality of the legal services our office provides. Thank you.

I had the pleasure of working with Attorney Brad Willard, and I can’t say enough about his professionalism and integrity. From the very beginning, he clearly explained the process, set realistic expectations, and made sure I understood both the strengths and potential challenges of my case. He didn’t overpromise—he gave straightforward, honest guidance, which I truly appreciated.

Brad is extremely responsive and detail-oriented. He takes the time to listen, answers questions thoroughly, and makes you feel like your case genuinely matters. His communication is consistent and clear, which reduces stress and builds confidence during what can be a very uncertain time.

Beyond his legal knowledge, what stands out most is his calm, steady approach and strong work ethic. He is organized, prepared, and always professional in his interactions. I felt supported every step of the way.

reviews.text:
I can’t recommend Avera & Smith enough. Alexandria Avera treated me like a person and not just a file. I was in a really bad car accident that put me into a coma and left me with severe injuries. I initially hired another local law firm. When that firm learned that the at-fault driver only had $10,000.00 in insurance coverage, they brought me that check at the hospital . I declined that and they withdrew from my case. But Alex promised to look closer and she did, discovering a different theory and uncovering $2 million in insurance coverage. They did a deep dive into the accident and what happened leading up to it, especially how it was that a minor became drunk before getting behind the wheel of his car. This investigation resulted in their recovering an additional $2,000,000.00 for me – something that will forever change my life and make all the difference in my recovery. Alex and her team kept in touch with me every step of the way to make sure I knew what was going on with my case. The at-fault driver was criminally charged and they made sure to help me with that process and be there for me when I had to go to court for his criminal DUI case. Once my case with them was resolved, they sat down with me with a financial advisor to make sure I knew about all my options to make sure I would be taken care of for the rest of my life.
If you are injured in an accident, you very much need to consider carefully your options and hire Alex and her team at Avera & Smith. Had my family not made that phone call, I would be bankrupt from medical bills and in a really bad place. Thank you, Alex!
reviews.ownerResponse: Wow, thank you Bradly. We’re incredibly grateful you shared your experience, especially in so much detail. From the beginning, Alex and her team knew there was more to uncover with your case. And she didn’t stop digging. What you went through was horrible and life-changing, to say the least. And, well, you deserved a team that would fight like h#@* to make sure you weren't the victim yet again of the insurance company. We’re proud of the work Alex and the team did. And we’re honored you trusted us when it counted most. We wish you continued healing and strength moving forward. Thank you again!

reviews.text:
I am editing my review, well actually adding to my previous review. I once again need the services of my attorney
Brad Willard. This time it is over a Radon issue that former client believes I have responsibility for. I brought this to Mr. Willard and he agreed to once again represent me. Although out of "HIS STATED" area of practice, he dived right into his. Let me tell you, Brad jumped right in. The level of research he had to do to respond to the opposite side is stunning. This man is a "MASTER" litigator. I do not say this lightly. Brad should advertise his skills as a Bomb Disposal & Demolition Expert. I really do feel sorry for the opposite side.  If you are looking for an extremely well educated, well versed, and a MASTER litigator look no further. I have witnessed him systematically take apart the opposite counsel several times now. I am telling you, do not get on the opposite side of this man. You will regret it.

We, (my family & me) found ourselves embroiled in a nasty dispute with a local neighbor in a very rural neighborhood. We purchased almost 24 acres more than 17 years ago to escape the confines of HOA' rules and regulations and nosy neighbors. We are ardent second amendment supporters, and we like our ATV's and Dirt Bikes. Hence, we bought enough property so we could enjoy our type of lifestyle. After more 16 years of doing just this without any interference, newcomers to the neighborhood moved in across the street and abruptly advised us that both our dirt bikes and guns were disturbing them and we needed to cease and desist our lifestyle. Footnote: We keep our property in pristine condition. This culminated in the neighbors calling the Marion County Sheriff's Office on us virtually every time we shoot our guns (more than 20 times). Notwithstanding, their property is more than 1700 feet or 3 full football fields away from where we shoot and in the opposite direction. This ultimately resulted in a repeat violence and stalking allegations filed against us in court.
We hired Mr. Brad Willard as our attorney. Where do I begin?
1. Attentive 2. Talented 3. Available on a moment’s notice 4. Detailed oriented. 5. He listens to you.
But more than all of that, he literally destroyed our neighbor’s testimony. I mean DESTROYED our neighbor when he got him on the witness stand. He exposed his lies, his made-up stories, and exposed him for exactly what he was trying to do. Our neighbor tried bringing New York City values and gun laws to rural Marion County. Mr. Willard protected us from him and those restrictions and for that we are most grateful to him and his law firm. Highly respected and highly recommend. Mr. Willard is a true skilled trial attorney and brings a wealth of experience to his clients. I would not like to be on the other side of him in a court room!

The Beach Family
reviews.ownerResponse:
John, thank you so much for your kind words. This case will always stick with me. I wish you all the best and I will always be here for you if you need me—but hopefully you won’t!
Sincerely,
Brad

Lance, Dana & Jenny demonstrated genuine care for my situation. They kept me informed throughout the entire process. It was obvious this wasn’t just a case number – they treated me like family.
Thanks to their tireless efforts, I received comprehensive medical treatment that helped me recover, plus all the financial benefits I deserved. The security this provided during such a difficult time was invaluable.
If you’re dealing with a workers’ compensation case, don’t hesitate to contact Lance Avera. Their combination of legal expertise, genuine compassion, and relentless advocacy makes them the clear choice for anyone who wants the best possible outcome for their case.

I went to Mr Moreno with a simple probate for my father   Mr Moreno explained since he had been dead for and few years this would be quite simple.
Mr Moreno prepared the paperwork while I was in his office. I paid him $750. He said it could take 2-3 months but with Covid possible longer. My check was cashed on 11/20/2020.
I waited 5 months before I started calling Mr Moreno’s office weekly. It took him 4 weeks to return my call. He told me in May that he would check with the court on the hold up. If he had indeed done that he would have realized he never filed the paperwork .
I was told by another attorney in that office that they were gonna prioritize my case and once filed I could receive the probate in 10-14 days. It has been over a month with no word from the court or his office.
Love this practice!

reviews.text:
Our experience with this firm has been disappointing and frustrating. We worked with them for several months on handling mechanic’s liens, expecting clarity and efficiency, but instead found that they often spent hours on unnecessary research without confirming whether we even wanted them to proceed. That approach drove up costs and wasted time.

What’s worse, we were misled about lien timing requirements. After following the process they previously said was acceptable, they later claimed we had “lost our lien rights” due to timing—something that could have been prevented with proactive communication or clearer guidance early on.

When concerns were raised, both David and Aymen were dismissive and defensive, showing little humility or accountability for their part in the confusion. It often felt like they viewed their work as flawless rather than being open to collaboration or feedback.

If you’re considering this firm, be very careful to confirm what you’re authorizing them to research or bill for. And make sure you fully understand their timelines and assumptions—because they won’t take responsibility if their guidance causes issues later.
Outstanding Law Firm. Don, Chris, and Aymen- Fantastic to work with. They are very knowledgeable and always kept us informed. If I could give them 10 stars I would.

Within months, Worthy had brought our case to mediation, and we had a resolution that provided us the legal ability and resources to move forward with our lives.  Worthy treated us with respect; he answered our questions promptly; he demonstrated a deep understanding of the principles of construction law. He has a thorough knowledge of the lawyers, judges, mediators, and contractors in the area which, in our case, helped a great deal.  Because he has been a lawyer working in Florida for several decades, he has a deep understanding  of the history of construction and construction law in Florida.  We recommend David without reservation. You can have confidence that he will give you his full attention, determine the best strategy for resolving your case, and execute it successfully.

reviews.text:
McCabe Harrison was recommended to us by several highly regarded attorneys in Gainesville Florida. Once we met him, we had a good sense of why they recommended him. After going through extensive estate considerations, with trustees and beneficiaries living in several states, we came to realize just how good he is.

Among other things, he instructed us about the clawback taxes for beneficiaries in the state of California, and how those taxes could affect beneficiaries we had to live in other states. He was willing to write verbiage in our revocable trusts to address the complex issues that California raised. He suggested that we could eliminate 95% of the problems by never having an executor who lived in the state of California, so we changed our named executors.

Because some of our beneficiaries were prior employees who we had not seen in many years, he pointed out that leaving percentages of our estate to any beneficiary, allowed them to challenge the provisions of our wills. The simple answer he offered was to specifies specific amounts for each beneficiary. Then we just had to make provision for what happens to any residual amounts, Weather higher or lower than our estimated dollar amounts. Again, this avoided a huge potential problem in making sure that the provisions of our wills and trust were nearly impossible to protest by beneficiaries who felt they should have gotten more.

He also pointed out that the wording of every operating agreement of any LLC we owned would take precedence over the provisions of our wills. He kindly directed us to another good attorney who could work in concert with Mr. Harrison to make sure that our operating agreements of any LLC we owned did not interfere with the provisions of our wills.

While all of that was very complex and required a significant amount of additional work for him, he never charged us anything beyond the initial check. I gave him at our first meeting.

Win his final paperwork came to us, it was close to 100 pages long. Rather than reviewing those by myself, my wife and I made an appointment to meet with him so he could explain in detail everything contained in that paperwork.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much, Larry! We truly appreciate you taking the time to share such a detailed and thoughtful review. It means a great deal to hear that McCabe and our team were able to guide you through complex estate planning matters and provide clarity and peace of mind. McCabe takes great pride in ensuring every client fully understands their plan and the reasoning behind each recommendation, so it’s wonderful to know that this approach made a difference for you and your wife. We’re honored by your trust and so glad the process was a positive experience. If you ever need anything in the future, we’re always here to help.

reviews.text: My mom has short-term memory loss, and I was trying to make an appointment for her. I was going to go with her, and I told them that they could call her power of attorney, her brother who resides in Washington State, regarding my attendance. The receptionist put me on hold I guess to speak to the attorney. When the receptionist returned, he said that the attorney stated that he does not do this kind of law…?!? So the attorney basically stated that he would not be able to provide us his services because he doesn’t do estate law. Even though his business is called Harrison  law. If you do not work with certain situations, then have the courage, integrity, professionalism, and empathy to tell that person so instead of providing a flat out lie. How belittling, helpless  and demeaning this experience has left me. This just reconfirms what we’ve seen, that legal representation is only available to select types of persons.
reviews.ownerResponse: I am sorry to hear you were not satisfied with your experience with our firm. In a time and age when we have millions of so called degrees hanging on the walls of offices. It is a delight to meet a person who depicts the value of this degree.
I am an Indian citizen who got caught up in domestic difficulty leading to a child custody case when I was in Gainesville, Florida. USA.
I found myself in a very helpless situation where the land and language was foreign to me.
As I struggled to get my baby back from my husband who had kidnapped her from our house when I was in the  shower.

The government of India, came to my rescue and hired Mr. Lawrence Marafino to represent me in my child custody case.

Marraffino Law Firm is one of the  Best Family attorney. This was a gift of God to me.

Mr Lawrence Marafino did an excellent job in representing me in court. He and his law team also encouraged me and gave  moral support as every day was a such a challenge for me.

By the Grace of God with their hard work, we won the case and I have my baby girl back with me.
As I faced this terrible challenge I learnt that has many Angels in this world. They walk the earth in human forms. One of them is Mr. Lawrence Marafino.

Now my little baby is with me in my country India

May God bless him and his very precious wife Ellen

Mr. Lawrence Marraffino is extremely knowledgeable and thorough in evaluating the case details. He provided me with valuable suggestions and approaches on how to go about my case.  He is very quick in response to all your questions and concerns. Ms Ellen his dear wife works very diligently on behalf of her client and knows the law. Her personality helps because she is also realistic and is concerned about the emotional issues of the case.

Lashanda another very valuable team member helped me a lot because it was very difficult for me to understand the Law system of the different Country. She helped me in making the right decisions when related to the matters of my country.

It was very difficult time for me because I was away without my daughter for 345 days but when I arrived in the US. I was able to meet my daughter and she was able to stay with me as per visitation agreement, they knew the best way to approach each situation. They were very thorough and made sure I was comfortable with the end result.

If you choose Mr. Marafino, you will be amazed with his professionalism and positive attitude. I would highly recommend Mr. Lawrence Marraffino  and his firm to anyone who is looking for the an outstanding with great human values that practices Family Law.

I called 3 rivers 2 1/2 yrs ago about Ieper plan school issue and unfair treatment of my daughter, Kierstyn, with the school and school board. My case was assigned to Mrs Beverly brown out of Jacksonville fl. She got Kierstyn home school tutoring and when that didn’t work, she opened doors for her to attend an alternative school. She kept me informed with her conversations with school and school board members. She was fiesta, aggressive and demanding when it came to them being on time with paperwork and information needed. I spoke with her in Aug. 2023and she told me that she was waiting to send out supeonas for all who were involved. I called 3rivers last month because I hadn’t heard from her and I was told that she resigned. Left a message for a return call. Called today and they tell me that my case was closed last may and they have no lawyers for education. So does this mean, after almost two years of hard work, dedication, money spent, time given etc. that my LOOKING GREAT case is over? I’m waiting on a return call from (Angie). No one ever returns my calls but Ms Beverly brown who has resigned. I’m very disappointed. Kierstyns school, school board, and now her lawyer has failed her. I HIGHLY recommend Mr Rabin. He is an absolute shark and 10000% beyond what you'd expect.  We changed a law together, kind of sorta. Really Mr Rabin and the 3Rivers team did it all and I signed and asked a bunch of questions repeatedly. But it's a law that hopefully will protect the citizens of Gainesville for years to come and give everyone a fair chance at housing.
Really this is the best legal aid I've ever had the pleasure of dealing with and I find myself often comparing other legal aid branches with this one.. no comparison hands down three rivers is the best.

2023 Update: I actually moved out of Mr Rabins area a long time ago, so I thought that was that. I ended up working with Disability Rights of Florida on another discrimination case involving housing and disability. Both attorneys consulted with Mr Rabin as the "state housing expert" especially when it comes to subsided housing programs such as Section 8 or Housing Choice, public housing and low income housing tax credit law. When I say multiple attorneys in multiple groups have consulted with him, I truly mean it they were entirely unaware of my previous interactions with the group and he remained their go do, despite being in an entirely different 'group' or practice, with many of their own specialized attorneys. Mr Rabin appears to be THE housing policy and rights legal expert of the state of Florida. I have no idea how Three Rivers managed to acquire such talent but it truly is remarkable, they are remarkable. As someone with extensive involvement with attorneys my entire life- having been an adopted foster child- and I have yet to encounter another attorney (much less a "free" one) who knows the amount of housing policy they do. This is by far the best legal aid I've ever encountered in any state and truly had the best staff. This agency has helped make housing in Gainesville and Alachua County accessible to more, giving rights the state doesn't that are just basic human dignity. This legal aid and its workers deserve far more than they get, recognition and otherwise.

Thanks to Mr Rabin and some wonderful attorneysat FL DisabilityRights , I've now effectually changed the housing rights and policies of two major cities in Florida. That helps all of us.  This particular legal aid truly will help you if they can. Remember to vote for more funding for this group and others like it!

reviews.text:
When my situation seem impossible - ongoing for 5 years and the last 4 years had been hell. My daughter was alienated from me. I have almost lost all hope of ever seeing my daughter again.

By a miracle, I found Jennifer. As she sat and listened to my story, I felt immediately comfortable with her. She is honest. She is strong willed. She appear meek and gentle but when it's time for her to do her job in courtroom, she's a whole different woman on a whole different level. You think lawyers you've had have the brains. She has the brains and the strength. Plus a heart. She does things the right way while sharks cheat. Her way is longer and more frustrating but it brings sweeter rewards. We won our custody battle  - we did it the right way, the best way to go. She has never steered me wrong when lawyers I had in the past failed to no fault of their own.

If you want the best chess player of the law. It's her. If you want a wonder woman  of the law, it's her. If you want a hero for your child, it's her. If you want the truth exposed, it's her. If you want your child home, it's her. If you want your innocence proven, it's her. If you want a bulldog that never let go, it's her. If you want an honest lawyer that will fight with all her might for justice, it's her.

Jennifer Kipke is the most humble lawyer I've ever known with 100% good intentions.

You won't regret hiring her. I will recommend her to anyone who needs help.

All she needs from you is to trust her. Let her do her job. And most important of all, be absolutely honest with her.

Trust me.

She's the one. She's not a quitter. She never give up.

My daughter and I owe so much to this amazing lady and her sidekick

reviews.text:
Exactly one year ago, I hired Miller Elder Law to help me move my mother, from her condo near Tampa, to a nursing home in Gainesville.

In an incredibly complicated situation, my mother was in the hospital, not at her condo, my only family member, normally living in Gainesville, embarked the very same week, on a 12 month round-the-world trip...and I was in the process of moving from Massachusetts to North Carolina! I needed to sell my mother's condo and get her moved. To say there were many challenges is an understatement.

The entire team at M.E.L. walked me through every detail - and they were one phone call or email away from my next request.

I simply could not have managed the entire transition of moving my mother from Tampa, to a nursing home in Gainesville- without their PATIENT and professional assistance! Every resource they provided, outside their scope of legal management, was top notch!

From the very first consultation to this months "personal check- in" with my mother, in her nursing home in Gainesville, working with M.E.L. has been an exemplary experience!

My mother is now happily living in a top quality nursing home in Gainesville, the legalities of her estate have been managed, and all medical paperwork has been completed and filed - everything is in place!
Truly, thank God for the entire team at Miller Elder Law!
reviews.ownerResponse: Many thanks Valerie for taking the time to share your experience. We are all very happy to be able to help you through this process-it takes a village! And we are thankful for you and your mom and glad we could work through this together!

We always felt like we were in great hands and are so happy we made the decision to plan our estate with the Miller Law Firm. The entire process was smooth and stress-free.

I would highly recommend the Miller Elder Law Firm.  They recently redid my Trust, which specifically addressed the special needs of my daughter.  As part of the estate planning package, they updating my Will, DPOA, Living Will, Health Care Surrogate and other documents.
I particularly appreciated the time the paralegal and attorney devoted to answering my questions and to adjusting the documents to reflect my wishes.
At the end of the process, they gave me a wonderfully organized estate planning notebook that included all the documents they had prepared as well as adding helpful instruction sheets my successor trustee, DPOA and health care surrogate might find helpful.
The whole process was completed in a timely manner while still being very attentive to addressing all my concerns and making sure I was completely satisfied with the final documents.

reviews.text:
The Miller Elder Law Firm has been extremely helpful and patient in the process of creating my Revocable Trust. I have asked many detailed questions and have received detailed answers over the last two years. I am a retired linguist and so have a particular interest in understanding the implications of the language of the trust documents that I signed.

Ashley Fry, in particular, has educated me in "trust language and terminology" by annotating digital drafts with useful explanations. She has also been careful to clarify which questions she can answer and which questions should be referred to Shannon Miller for explanation.

Both Shannon Miller and Ashley Fry have also taken great care to respond to and clarify my wishes with respect to my husband's family in Peru. I appreciate very much their attention to detail, even in seemingly small matters such as making sure that my Peruvian relatives' names are cited and spelled correctly.

I am extremely grateful to have been educated and supported so well in planning for my future by creating this trust.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are so happy to help you Mary and glad we could take care of you and your family. One of the things we pride ourselves on is non-lawyer speak so everyone can understand the sometimes complicated language. Thank you for choosing us.

reviews.text: I see most of the negative reviews talk about how they just want your money and I can assure anyone that those reviews are absolutely correct. I Called them about a case regarding my dead father's estate. I explained that I have a son undergoing chemotherapy and couldn't afford much money. I spoke to and met with Mr. Knellinger himself with my son present. He said and I quote "I don't want to take a dime from you if I can't do nothing for you". After meeting him in his office I knew that there was no chance he would get me anything back from my father's estate. So I emailed saying I wanted my money back and to close my case. I received the check and a itemized bill for every phone call including the initial call before I was technically a client. The bill also included the meeting, reviewing paperwork that I gave them so they wouldn't have to get it themselves, and any other conversations by any means we had. How can you charge someone for an initial phone call before you even know they will be a client! If I would have never called back I would have never been charged for anything. The bad reviews are real and this is definitely not the Law office you want representing you.

“Rick Knellinger is personable, professional, and most importantly, patient – all essential qualities in a trust attorney. Rick gets my highest reccomendation.”

Linda Blondheim
Gainesville, FL

If they cannot discuss the case with me, fine (even though I know I was authorized at one point to receive information). Not returning phone calls is unacceptable.

Either "unavailable" or "in a meeting".

You have done absolutely nothing but copy information onto an already filled out document that any human being can do themselves and spent less than 3 minutes at a courthouse. Did many things we asked you not to do and wasted our time and yours but want to say you used $4500 and charging for more time. I would say to anyone interested here to beware what they are getting themselves into.

reviews.text:
After my mother-in-law passed away we contacted one of the attorneys that had prepared some of the will documents asking for some advice. My husband was promptly told that we would need to pay a $6,000 retainer first then they would look into it. Needless to say we didn’t send them a dime.
A few years passed while we tried to get some help on how to find an attorney to give us some advice with no luck. Living in the North East while my MIL lived in Florida complicated things. Once again I tried to find some advice about wills and probate in Florida and came across an in-depth article about how estate law can be handled. This article was written by the David’s - a law group that only handles wills and estates in Gainesville FL. After learning so much from the website my husband and I agreed to call.
Our call was late on a Friday afternoon and after speaking with the lovely receptionist she set us up with a phone call with the staff member who handles the initial intake. We were surprised to hear from him mid-morning on Monday. We told him about our mother’s will and the issues created by waiting so long to close it out. He assured us that the handling of the will would be easier than we expected and arranged for a free consultation with Mr. David an hour later.
Mr. David called and gave us advice. Finding out there was a trust in place and no other beneficiaries involved he told us to just contact the bank to have them put the accounts in my husband’s name. After a 15 minute call we were told we didn’t need to go through probate and didn’t even need a lawyer to handle the accounts transfer.
We were shocked. Something we thought would be a problem handling, now was so simple.
We haven’t taken the next step with the bank yet but I can’t thank him enough for the free advice. We are so relieved. Definitely contact this practice for help - they are wonderful people!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for the review! Glad we could help!
Thanks!

I lost my husband in February of 2024.  Unfortunately, he did not provide beneficiary information on every account of his and so the probate process was necessary. I also did not like the reviews of some of the other probate attorneys in Southeast Florida.  From the outset I felt at ease with Nathan.  He has a calm, cool, and collected demeanor and a wonderful ability to instill confidence and put me at ease.  Nathan performed beyond my expectations and throughout the process he was extremely patient with me.  I am not a technical person, and so every communication with him requiring signatures and documents I sent via US Postal Service, and he would send back things via US Postal Service.  He was thorough in his work and in his projected timeline(s) to getting things done.  Everything occurred just as he said it would when he said it would.  He was extremely prompt in communications and returning calls and emails; most times he was there to pick up the phone!  I am grateful to him and would urge anyone in need of his services to contact him first and save themselves a lot of unnecessary worry.
